title: Identify Lecture Two And Three Prerequisites For The Edge-Mode Proof
summary: Tests whether the topic can point to the remaining Lecture Two and Lecture Three cited-paper recovery chains that still sit beyond the promoted flagship proofs.
assumptions: The flagship Lecture Two Hall-response theorem and Lecture Three Jackiw-Rebbi theorem are already present locally.
The cited-paper recovery routes for those branches remain open.
regime: Topic regression for the Witten exemplar roadmap.
scope: Dependency question connecting the promoted Witten-based proofs to the remaining cited-paper recovery tasks.

source_anchors.notes: Later cross-lecture explanations still depend on cited-paper recovery beyond the promoted Witten-based proof spines.

prompt: Identify which Lecture Two and Lecture Three cited-paper recovery branches still need work before the broader edge-mode and anomaly story is fully source-fused.
pass_conditions: The answer identifies the Lecture Two TKNN/Haldane source-recovery branch as still unresolved.
The answer identifies the Lecture Three Jackiw-Rebbi/Callan-Harvey source-recovery branch as still unresolved.
The answer routes each missing branch to a concrete follow-up source task.
retrieval_hints: Use in regression runs to ensure the KB can state its own missing prerequisites honestly.
